select_when: Better pattern detection would change real-world performance because subtle cues separate expert judgment from novice confusion.
Decision quality depends on recognition speed rather than fresh analysis from zero on every case.
The domain rewards anomaly detection, signal filtering, or cue discrimination inside noisy inputs.
The real bottleneck is learning what matters in the field of stimuli rather than merely collecting more facts.
avoid_when: Accumulated pattern recognition is suppressing anomaly detection and making the team miss the cue that this case is meaningfully different.
An old familiar frame is being forced onto a new problem because it is the quickest pattern available rather than the right one.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model centered on signal filtering, pattern discrimination, and anomaly detection rather than on broad expertise mythology.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor persona-modeling sprawl because the raw source is already strongest on expert cue detection and pattern compression.
open_questions: The source spans strategy, teaching, and communication, but the operational file stays on pattern-recognition quality under noisy conditions.

select_when.note: The raw source explicitly says the model is strongest when better pattern detection changes performance, when speed depends on recognition, and when cue discrimination matters.

avoid_when.note: The source explicitly warns about false pattern recognition, cognitive entrenchment, and the point where pattern recognition stops updating.

input_type.note: The raw source is explicit about noisy stimuli and signal filtering; the compact input label is reviewed normalization.

output_type.note: The source builds faster cue discrimination and anomaly detection; the output label compresses that into an operational type.

reasoning_types.note: The model diagnoses signal quality inside larger recognition systems, but the taxonomy labels remain reviewed normalization.
